title: A Jedi's Weapon

"'''A Jedi's Weapon'''" is a [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] featured in [[Free Comic Book Day 2002 Special]] and [[Star Wars Tales 12|''Star Wars Tales'' 12]].

==Plot summary==
The story chronicles a diplomatic mission of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. On [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], they meet King [[Sha-Mar Ma-dred]] from [[Kashir]] who wishes to join the Republic, and his four (dramatically attractive) wives. After the initial meeting, Anakin remarks that the [[Kashirim]] seem very friendly, and he doesn't know why Obi-Wan warned him to be on his guard. Obi-Wan archly informs him that Kashirim are renowned throughout the galaxy for their expertise at [[Thief/Legends|thievery]], which they regard as an important social skill. While Ma-Dred's wives exit the room where the negotiations take place, Anakin is distracted by the wives' and his [[Anakin Skywalker's first lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] is stolen.

Anakin embarks on a mission to recover the lightsaber before Obi-Wan finds out about it. He is led from the first three wives to the last one, [[Ashala Ma-dred|Ashala]], who is next to the sleeping king in his room. She reveals that she lost it shortly after the theft, but Anakin is forced to dangle out a nearby window when guards intrude inside the room. Eventually, Anakin gives up and begins to trudge back to his room when he finds Kenobi. The [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] lectures Anakin about the importance of his lightsaber, and hands it back. After he witnessed the wife's theft, he gently used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to recover it. Taken aback, Anakin asks why Obi-Wan didn't say anything, and Obi-Wan lectures that it is just as important for there to be candor and trust between a Master and his [[Padawan]]. Obi-Wan was only waiting for Anakin to come back and admit that he'd lost his lightsaber. In the same spirit of candor, Anakin uncomfortably starts to confess the lengths he went to to retrieve his lightsaber, and Obi-Wan is predictably outraged.

==Continuity==
Though this is a ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' story that was released before [[Star Wars Tales 21|''Star Wars Tales'' 21]] and was therefore originally considered [[Infinities]], a reference in ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' places the basic storyline into the main [[canon]].
